stem: A company uses Amazon S3 to host their website and Amazon EC2 for application servers. Under the Shared Responsibility Model, which task is the customer responsible for that AWS is NOT?

options.label: A
options.text: Ensuring S3 service availability across Availability Zones

options.label: B
options.text: Maintaining the global AWS network backbone

options.label: C
options.text: Ensuring the physical servers running EC2 are not tampered with

options.label: D
options.text: Replacing failed storage drives in S3 infrastructure

options.label: E
options.text: Applying security patches to the operating system on EC2 instances

explanation: OS patching on EC2 instances is the customer's responsibility. AWS provides the EC2 service and the underlying hardware, but the guest operating system is under the customer's control — including patching, configuration, and hardening.

stem: A new developer joins a company and needs read-only access to S3 and the ability to describe EC2 instances. What is the best way to grant this access?

options.label: A
options.text: Grant the developer access by adding their email to the AWS account

options.label: B
options.text: Share the root account credentials and ask them to be careful

options.label: C
options.text: Create an IAM user and attach an inline policy with AdministratorAccess

options.label: D
options.text: Add the developer to the Administrators IAM group

options.label: E
options.text: Create an IAM user and attach AWS managed policies for AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess and AmazonEC2ReadOnlyAccess

explanation: The principle of least privilege is applied by creating an IAM user and attaching only the specific AWS managed policies that match what the developer needs. AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess and AmazonEC2ReadOnlyAccess grant exactly the permissions described.

stem: A company wants to enforce that all IAM users must use multi-factor authentication to access the AWS Management Console. How can they enforce this at scale?

options.label: A
options.text: Email all users asking them to enable MFA voluntarily

options.label: B
options.text: Require users to log in through a VPN which enforces MFA

options.label: C
options.text: Create an IAM policy that denies all console actions unless MFA is

options.label: D
options.text: Enable MFA only for the root account

options.label: E
options.text: Use AWS Shield Advanced to enforce MFA on console logins

explanation: An IAM policy with a condition 'aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent: false → Deny' prevents actions in the console unless the user authenticated with MFA. Attached to all users or via an SCP, this enforces MFA organization-wide.

stem: A Lambda function needs to read items from a DynamoDB table. What is the most secure way to give the function access?

options.label: A
options.text: Make the DynamoDB table publicly accessible

options.label: B
options.text: Hard-code AWS access keys in the Lambda function environment variables

options.label: C
options.text: Store access keys in an S3 bucket and load them at function startup

options.label: D
options.text: Create an IAM user with DynamoDB access and pass the credentials via the function payload

options.label: E
options.text: Create an IAM role with DynamoDB read permissions and assign it as the Lambda execution role

explanation: IAM roles assigned to Lambda functions provide temporary credentials automatically via the execution environment. The function can call DynamoDB without any credentials in code or configuration — AWS handles credential injection securely.

stem: A penetration tester finds that a web application behind an ALB is vulnerable to SQL injection. The team wants to add a protection layer that filters and blocks malicious HTTP requests without modifying the application. Which service should they deploy?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon GuardDuty — detect SQL injection attempts

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Shield Standard — block layer 3/4 attacks

options.label: C
options.text: AWS WAF with SQL injection match rules deployed on the ALB

options.label: D
options.text: Security groups — restrict inbound traffic to port 443 only

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Inspector — scan the application for SQL injection vulnerabilities

explanation: AWS WAF can be attached to an ALB, CloudFront, or API Gateway. Managed rule groups for SQL injection inspection filter HTTP requests and block those containing injection patterns before they reach the application.

stem: A company's S3 bucket that stores employee PII was recently made public by accident. Which service would have detected this within minutes and alerted the security team?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Shield — monitors S3 for DDoS against public buckets

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor — checks S3 bucket permissions as part of security checks

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Macie — detects PII but not bucket permission changes

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Config with a rule detecting publicly accessible S3 buckets

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Inspector — scans S3 buckets for security vulnerabilities

explanation: AWS Config continuously monitors resource configurations and evaluates them against rules. The 's3-bucket-public-read-prohibited' managed rule triggers a noncompliance finding immediately when a bucket is made public, enabling rapid notification.

stem: An organization needs to prove to a healthcare regulator that their AWS infrastructure meets HIPAA requirements. What does AWS provide to support this?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS enables HIPAA-eligible services and provides a BAA (Business Associate Agreement).

options.label: B
options.text: AWS takes full legal responsibility for HIPAA compliance on behalf of customers

options.label: C
options.text: Customers must move to AWS GovCloud for all HIPAA workloads

options.label: D
options.text: AWS provides a HIPAA compliance checklist through Trusted Advisor

options.label: E
options.text: AWS guarantees HIPAA compliance for all customer workloads automatically

explanation: AWS offers a BAA for HIPAA-eligible services under the Shared Responsibility Model. AWS's infrastructure is compliant-eligible, but the customer must configure and operate their workloads correctly and sign the BAA — available through AWS Artifact.

stem: A security engineer runs a script that calls DeleteBucket on multiple S3 buckets. The next morning, a manager asks who deleted the buckets and when. Which service provides this information?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Config — records the S3 bucket configuration before deletion

options.label: B
options.text: AWS CloudTrail — records the DeleteBucket API call including caller identity and timestamp

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Macie — classifies data in deleted buckets

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon S3 Server Access Logs — logs each S3 API call to that bucket

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon CloudWatch — logs of all AWS service metrics

explanation: CloudTrail captures every API call including DeleteBucket, the IAM identity that made it, the timestamp, and the source IP. Even after a bucket is deleted, the CloudTrail event record persists in the CloudTrail log files.

stem: An IAM user attempts to launch an EC2 instance but receives an 'Access Denied' error despite having an EC2FullAccess policy attached. What is the most likely cause?

options.label: A
options.text: EC2 is temporarily unavailable in that Region

options.label: B
options.text: EC2FullAccess only covers read operations, not launch

options.label: C
options.text: An AWS Organizations Service Control Policy (SCP) is explicitly denying

options.label: D
options.text: The user's MFA token has expired

options.label: E
options.text: The user needs to be in the Administrators group to launch EC2

explanation: SCPs in AWS Organizations set the maximum permissions boundary for all accounts in the organization. An SCP with an explicit Deny overrides any IAM policy Allow — the user has EC2FullAccess but the SCP prevents the action at the account level.

stem: A company wants to ensure all data stored in Amazon EBS volumes is encrypted. Which option enforces this for all newly created EBS volumes in an account?

options.label: A
options.text: Use Amazon Inspector to scan all EBS volumes for encryption status

options.label: B
options.text: Create a KMS key and manually encrypt each EBS volume after it is created

options.label: C
options.text: Enable EBS encryption by default at the account/region level

options.label: D
options.text: Use AWS Config to detect unencrypted volumes and alert the security team

options.label: E
options.text: Create an IAM policy that prevents users from creating unencrypted volumes

explanation: Enabling 'EBS encryption by default' in the EC2 console settings ensures all new EBS volumes are automatically encrypted using the default KMS key, regardless of how the volume is created — no manual steps required.

stem: An EC2 instance in a private subnet needs to download software updates from the internet without receiving inbound connections from the internet. Which component enables outbound-only internet access?

options.label: A
options.text: NAT Gateway — deployed in a public subnet, routes outbound traffic

options.label: B
options.text: Elastic IP address — assigned to the instance

options.label: C
options.text: VPC Peering — connects to a VPC with internet access

options.label: D
options.text: Internet Gateway — attached to the VPC

options.label: E
options.text: Direct Connect — provides a dedicated private connection to the internet

explanation: A NAT Gateway in a public subnet provides one-way outbound internet access for private subnet instances. Instances initiate outbound connections through NAT; the NAT Gateway translates the address — inbound connections initiated from the internet are blocked.

stem: A DevOps engineer needs to rotate the access keys for all IAM users every 90 days. Which AWS service can detect which users have not rotated their keys within that period?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Macie — identifies expired credentials in S3

options.label: B
options.text: AWS KMS — tracks all encryption key rotation schedules

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor — includes a security check for IAM access key rotation

options.label: D
options.text: AWS CloudTrail — shows the last time each key was used

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon GuardDuty — detects long-lived credentials being used maliciously

explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor includes an 'IAM Access Key Rotation' check (available on Business Support and above) that identifies access keys older than 90 days — directly answering the rotation compliance question.

stem: A company hosts a public API on API Gateway backed by Lambda. They want to protect against a sudden traffic surge from a single IP that could exhaust Lambda concurrency. Which service layer should they add?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Config — detect Lambda concurrency limit changes

options.label: B
options.text: AWS WAF with rate-based rules attached to API Gateway

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Shield Advanced — protect against volumetric attacks

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon GuardDuty — detect the traffic anomaly

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Inspector — scan Lambda for vulnerabilities

explanation: AWS WAF rate-based rules count requests from a single IP and automatically block IPs that exceed a defined threshold. Deployed on API Gateway, this prevents a single client from overwhelming Lambda concurrency limits.

stem: A company's AWS account receives an unusual number of failed API calls targeting IAM from an unknown IP address at 2 AM. Which service would have automatically detected and alerted on this suspicious activity?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Inspector — scans IAM for misconfigurations

options.label: B
options.text: AWS WAF — blocks suspicious HTTP requests to the console

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor — checks for IAM security best practices

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon GuardDuty — uses ML to detect anomalous IAM activity from CloudTrail

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Config — detects IAM configuration changes

explanation: GuardDuty continuously analyzes CloudTrail management events for suspicious patterns like unusual IAM API call volumes, calls from anomalous IPs, or credential reconnaissance — and generates findings automatically without manual rule configuration.

stem: A financial institution stores transaction records in Amazon S3 for 7 years. Regulators require that records cannot be altered or deleted once written. Which S3 feature satisfies this requirement?

options.label: A
options.text: S3 Lifecycle policy — transitions objects to Glacier where deletion is harder

options.label: B
options.text: S3 Object Lock in Compliance mode — prevents deletion or overwrite for the duration of.

options.label: C
options.text: S3 Replication — copies are maintained in another bucket

options.label: D
options.text: S3 Versioning — previous versions are retained when objects are overwritten

options.label: E
options.text: S3 Bucket Policy — denies DeleteObject to all users

explanation: S3 Object Lock in Compliance mode prevents any user — including root — from deleting or overwriting an object for the specified retention period. This satisfies SEC 17a-4 and other regulations requiring immutable records.

stem: A company is building a multi-account AWS environment and wants to prevent accounts in the development OU from being able to launch EC2 instances in production VPCs. Which control mechanism provides this guardrail?

options.label: A
options.text: Network ACLs in the production subnets

options.label: B
options.text: VPC security groups in the production VPCs

options.label: C
options.text: CloudTrail alerts for cross-account EC2 launches

options.label: D
options.text: IAM policies in the development accounts

options.label: E
options.text: Service Control Policies (SCPs) applied to the development OU

explanation: SCPs applied to a dev OU can deny ec2:RunInstances in specific resource conditions (e.g., production VPC ARNs) or deny cross-account resource access entirely. SCPs are the preventive guardrail mechanism in AWS Organizations.

stem: A company's database password is stored in plain text in an EC2 launch configuration. A security review flags this as a critical risk. What is the correct remediation?

options.label: A
options.text: Encode the password in Base64 before storing it in the launch configuration

options.label: B
options.text: Restrict IAM permissions so fewer users can view the launch configuration

options.label: C
options.text: Store the database password in AWS Secrets Manager and have the application retrieve it

options.label: D
options.text: Enable CloudTrail to log whenever the launch configuration is accessed

options.label: E
options.text: Encrypt the EC2 EBS volume, which automatically encrypts all text on the volume

explanation: Secrets Manager stores secrets encrypted at rest and in transit. The application retrieves the secret at runtime using temporary credentials from its IAM role — the password is never stored in the launch configuration, source code, or any file.

stem: A company needs SSL/TLS certificates for their web application running on an Application Load Balancer. They want the certificates to renew automatically without operational overhead. Which service provides this?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS KMS — generates and manages SSL certificates

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Secrets Manager — stores SSL certificate private keys

options.label: C
options.text: AWS CloudHSM — stores certificates in dedicated hardware

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) — provisions and auto-renews free

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Inspector — validates certificate expiry

explanation: AWS Certificate Manager provides free SSL/TLS certificates for use with AWS services (ALB, CloudFront, API Gateway) and automatically renews them before expiry — no manual renewal process needed.

stem: A company's security policy requires that CloudTrail logs cannot be deleted by any IAM user, including administrators. Which combination of features enforces this?

options.label: A
options.text: Enable CloudWatch Logs integration — logs in CloudWatch cannot be deleted

options.label: B
options.text: Restrict CloudTrail delete permissions in IAM for all users

options.label: C
options.text: Store CloudTrail logs in S3 with a bucket policy denying DeleteObject — administrators can modify the policy

options.label: D
options.text: Use AWS Backup to back up CloudTrail logs every night

options.label: E
options.text: Store CloudTrail logs in S3 with Object Lock and enable CloudTrail log file integrity validation

explanation: S3 Object Lock in Compliance mode on the CloudTrail bucket prevents log deletion even by root. Log file integrity validation creates a hash of each log file — tampering with or deleting logs breaks the validation chain, providing both prevention and detection.

stem: A company is migrating a workload to AWS and has a compliance requirement for PCI DSS. The security team asks what AWS provides to demonstrate the infrastructure meets PCI DSS requirements. What should they use?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor PCI DSS check

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Inspector PCI DSS scan

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Artifact — provides the AWS PCI DSS

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Security Hub PCI DSS standard

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Config PCI DSS compliance pack

explanation: AWS Artifact is the self-service portal for AWS compliance documentation. It provides the AWS PCI DSS Attestation of Compliance (AOC), which demonstrates that AWS infrastructure is PCI DSS Level 1 certified — the highest level of assurance.

stem: An organization wants to use AWS KMS to encrypt their RDS database. They want the flexibility to rotate the encryption key annually without re-encrypting the database. How does AWS KMS support this?

options.label: A
options.text: Key rotation is handled automatically by RDS — KMS has no role in rotation

options.label: B
options.text: KMS does not support key rotation — a new key must be created and data re-encrypted manually

options.label: C
options.text: Enabling automatic key rotation in KMS rotates the backing key material annually while keeping the same Key ID

options.label: D
options.text: Key rotation requires the database to be taken offline for re-encryption

options.label: E
options.text: KMS key rotation changes the Key ID, which requires updating all services using the key

explanation: KMS automatic key rotation rotates the backing cryptographic material once per year while keeping the Key ID unchanged. Data encrypted with older material can still be decrypted using the same Key ID — KMS tracks which key material version was used for each encryption.

stem: A company discovers their AWS account has been used to mine cryptocurrency. GuardDuty generated a finding 3 days ago but no one acted on it. What process gap does this reveal?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS should have alerted the company about the mining activity

options.label: B
options.text: WAF should have blocked the cryptocurrency mining traffic

options.label: C
options.text: CloudTrail should have blocked the mining activity

options.label: D
options.text: The company lacks an incident response process

options.label: E
options.text: GuardDuty should have automatically terminated the mining instances

explanation: Detective controls (GuardDuty) did their job by generating a finding. The failure was in the response process — no one was monitoring findings and acting on them. Effective security requires detective controls AND an incident response process including alerting and automated or manual response procedures.

stem: A company's IAM group named 'Developers' has an attached policy that allows s3:PutObject. An individual developer also has a personal policy that denies s3:PutObject. What is the developer's effective permission?

options.label: A
options.text: Allow — the most permissive policy wins

options.label: B
options.text: Allow — the group policy takes precedence over individual policies

options.label: C
options.text: Deny — explicit Deny in any policy always overrides Allow

options.label: D
options.text: Allow — group policies override individual user policies

options.label: E
options.text: It depends on the order the policies were attached

explanation: IAM policy evaluation: an explicit Deny in any attached policy always overrides any Allow, regardless of whether the Deny comes from a user policy, group policy, or resource-based policy. The developer cannot perform s3:PutObject.

stem: A company needs a dedicated hardware security module to store cryptographic keys with the highest level of compliance (FIPS 140-2 Level 3). Which AWS service provides dedicated HSM hardware?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Certificate Manager

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Secrets Manager

options.label: C
options.text: AWS KMS with customer-managed keys

options.label: D
options.text: AWS CloudHSM

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon Macie

explanation: AWS CloudHSM provides dedicated single-tenant HSM hardware in your VPC. It meets FIPS 140-2 Level 3 requirements and gives you exclusive control over your keys — AWS cannot access the keys stored in CloudHSM.

stem: A company is implementing the principle of least privilege across their AWS accounts. Which TWO actions directly apply this principle? (Select TWO)

options.label: A
options.text: Replacing AdministratorAccess policies with scoped policies that grant only what each role needs

options.label: B
options.text: Enabling AWS CloudTrail in all Regions

options.label: C
options.text: Encrypting all EBS volumes with KMS

options.label: D
options.text: Using IAM Access Analyzer to identify and remove permissions that

options.label: E
options.text: Enabling MFA on the root account

explanation: Least privilege: (1) replace broad policies with scoped ones giving only necessary permissions; (2) use IAM Access Analyzer's unused access findings to identify and remove permissions that were granted but never exercised — continuously tightening the access model.

stem: A company wants to implement preventive security controls for their S3 data. Which TWO controls prevent unauthorized access before it occurs rather than detecting it after? (Select TWO)

options.label: A
options.text: Configuring S3 bucket policies to deny access to unauthorized principals

options.label: B
options.text: Enabling S3 Block Public Access at the account level

options.label: C
options.text: Configuring AWS Config to alert on public bucket changes

options.label: D
options.text: Enabling S3 Server Access Logging

options.label: E
options.text: Enabling Amazon Macie to scan for sensitive data

explanation: Preventive controls block actions before they occur. S3 Block Public Access prevents public ACLs and bucket policies from being applied — stopping the misconfiguration at the source. Bucket policies explicitly deny unauthorized principals — blocking access attempts before data is reached.

stem: A company's security policy requires both prevention and detection of infrastructure changes that could introduce risk. Which TWO services should they implement to address both requirements? (Select TWO)

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Redshift — stores infrastructure change records

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Rekognition — analyzes images in S3 for security threats

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Config with conformance packs — detects noncompliant resource configurations

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Organizations SCPs — prevents accounts from making unauthorized actions

options.label: E
options.text: Amazon SageMaker — builds ML models to predict infrastructure failures

explanation: SCPs prevent unauthorized actions (preventive) by enforcing boundaries on what accounts can do. AWS Config with conformance packs detects when deployed configurations drift from desired state (detective) — together they form a prevention + detection governance layer.
